title: Cheiloclinium pedunculatum
taxonomy: Cheiloclinium pedunculatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It belongs to the genus Cheiloclinium, which is a group of flowering plants. The plant's classification is as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Eudicots, Clade - Rosids, Order - Celastrales, Family - Celastraceae, Genus - Cheiloclinium, Species - C. pedunculatum. This classification helps scientists understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics. The plant's classification also helps with its identification and study in the field of botany.

intro: Cheiloclinium pedunculatum is a plant species commonly known as the "Pedunculate Cheiloclinium" or "Guayacanillo", belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of pain, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cheiloclinium pedunculatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritations.
